title: ranunculus eschscholtzii var. suksdorfii
intro: Ranunculus eschscholtzii var. suksdorfii, also known as the Mountain Buttercup, is a perennial plant species belonging to the buttercup family (Ranunculaceae) and is native to the Pacific Northwest region of North America. The Mountain Buttercup has been used in traditional medicine for its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, making it effective in treating wounds, reducing pain, and fighting infections. The plant's medicinal properties also provide various health benefits, including relieving digestive issues, reducing fever, and promoting respiratory health. Traditional herbal preparations of Mountain Buttercup include infusions, decoctions, and salves, which can be used topically or internally to harness its medicinal properties.
